meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
family.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 5. Occupations classified in TEER 5, as those included in this group with a 5 as the second digit of their code, are typically accessible with short work demonstration and no formal educational requirements.
family.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
family.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

units.title: Longshore workers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.definition: Longshore workers transfer cargo throughout dock area and onto and from ships and other vessels. They are employed by marine cargo handling companies, shipping agencies and shipping lines.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Longshore workers transfer cargo throughout dock area and onto and from ships and other vessels.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education may be required.
additional_information: Progression to foreman/woman or supervisor of longshore workers is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to conduct inspections of containers and cargo for damage, monitor adherence to safety regulations and document discrepancies to ensure compliance with shipping standards

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to observe machine operations to ensure proper functioning, detect malfunctions or unusual dock conditions and make basic adjustments or report issues for further action
to efficiently manage the speed and flow of cargo operations using various equipment and machinery and to quickly and accurately respond to dynamic conditions on the docks
to expertly synchronize the movement of multiple limbs to operate heavy machinery, balance loads and secure cargo, during complex tasks
to meticulously follow safety protocols, thoroughly inspect cargo for damage or irregularities, ensure precise documentation and carefully operate heavy machinery

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Vehicle, Machinery and Equipment Operations
core_competencies.statement: to understand operating and coordinating advanced cargo-handling equipment, such as gantry cranes and heavy-duty forklifts, while ensuring precise synchronization and safety in complex, large-scale port operations

units.title: Material handlers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.definition: Material handlers handle, move, load and unload materials by hand or using a variety of material handling equipment. They are employed by transportation, storage and moving companies, and by a variety of manufacturing and processing companies and retail and wholesale warehouses.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Material handlers (manual) handle, move, load and unload materials by hand.
main_duties: Load, unload and move products and materials by hand or using basic material handling equipment
Perform other material handling activities such as counting, weighing, sorting, packing and unpacking.
May perform other activities, such as opening containers and crates, filling warehouse orders, assisting in taking inventory and weighing and checking materials.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.
Physical strength is required for manual material handlers who work with heavy materials.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Material handlers (equipment operators) handle, move, load and unload materials by using a variety of material handling equipment.
main_duties: Move household appliances and furniture onto and off moving trucks or vans
Operate winches and other loading devices to load and unload materials onto and off trucks, railway cars and loading docks of warehouses and industrial establishments
Operate industrial trucks, tractors, loaders and other equipment to transport materials to and from transportation vehicles and loading docks and to store and retrieve materials in warehouses
Connect hoses or pipes and operate equipment to load and unload liquid petroleum, chemical or other products into or from tank cars, tank trucks or storage tanks
Operate equipment to dump materials such as coal, ore and grain into or to remove materials from railway cars, trucks or other vehicles
Operate conveyors and equipment to transfer grain or other materials from transportation vehicles to elevators, bins or other storage areas
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education may be required.

units.title: Construction trades helpers and labourers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.definition: Construction trades helpers and labourers assist skilled tradespersons and perform labouring activities at construction sites, in quarries and in surface mines. They are employed by construction companies, trade and labour contractors, and surface mine and quarry operators.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Construction trades helpers and labourers assist skilled tradespersons and perform labouring activities at construction sites, in quarries and in surface mines.
employment_requirements: Some experience as a general construction labourer may be required for construction trade helpers.
Some pipeline workers, such as stabbers, mandrel operators and pre-heater tenders, usually require one season of experience in oil and gas pipeline construction.
Flagmen/women may require a traffic control certificate.
Riggers and slingers may require a rigging certificate.
On-the-job training may be provided.
additional_information: Mobility is possible among workers in this unit group.
Progression from rigger to crane operator is possible with experience and additional certification.

example_titles.job_title_text: churn drill operator helper - water well
pile driver operator helper - crane operation
interior and exterior wall erector helper
drywall and acoustical board installer helper
vanity and kitchen cupboard installer helper

core_competencies.statement: to inspect materials, measure and check basic dimensions and ensure alignment with specified standards
to operate pneumatic hammers, vibrators, tampers and to tend machines and equipment such as mixers, compressors and pumps
to repeatedly exert maximum force, enabling them to handle heavy lifting, pulling, pushing of various construction objects and sustained physical tasks across varied job site conditions
to exert maximum core strength continuously to stabilize their bodies during heavy lifting, carrying and manual handling of construction materials and equipment
to demonstrate strong teamwork, clear communication and adaptability to efficiently support skilled tradespeople and address site needs

to understand safety protocols, using construction tools, handling and transporting materials, site layout, assisting skilled tradespeople, knowing construction terminology and performing site clean-up

units.title: Other trades helpers and labourers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.definition: Other trades helpers and labourers assist skilled tradespersons and perform labouring activities in the installation, maintenance and repair of industrial machinery, refrigeration, heating and air conditioning equipment, in the maintenance and repair of transportation and heavy equipment, in the installation and repair of telecommunication and power cables and in other repair and service work settings. They are employed by a wide variety of manufacturing, utility and service companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Other trades helpers and labourers assist skilled tradespersons and perform labouring activities in the installation, maintenance and repair of industrial machinery, refrigeration, heating and air conditioning equipment, in the maintenance and repair of transportation and heavy equipment, in the installation and repair of telecommunication and power cables and in other repair and service work settings.
employment_requirements: On-the-job training is provided.
Some secondary school education may be required.
additional_information: There is little or no mobility between occupations in this unit group.

example_titles.job_title_text: heating and air conditioning mechanic helper
electric power and wire communications labourer
surveying and optical instruments repairer helper
powered-machine or towed-machine repairer helper - construction
cable installation and repair helper - telecommunications
lineman/woman helper - power and communication lines

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to monitor and repair industrial machinery and equipment, refrigeration, heating and air-conditioning equipment, elevators and stationary electric-power generating and distribution equipment

to fix minor equipment issues and perform simple repairs on tools, along with preventative maintenance skills like regularly inspecting and cleaning tools and equipment to ensure proper functionality and safety

to move tools, equipment and other materials to and from work areas and assist in, for example, land-surveying activities such as holding and moving stakes, clearing debris and transporting surveying tools to work areas
to effectively perform tasks such as arranging heavy equipment parts in confined spaces, which requires bending, stretching and reaching while handling heavy materials
to effectively communicate with trades people and other coworkers, adapt to varying conditions and collaborate with team members to coordinate tasks
to follow safety protocols, use basic tools, handle materials, understand site layout, assist skilled workers, know construction terms and perform site clean-up

units.title: Taxi and limousine drivers and chauffeurs
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.minor_group_title: Taxi, personal service and delivery service drivers
units.definition: Taxi and limousine drivers drive automobiles and limousines to transport passengers. Chauffeurs drive automobiles and limousines to transport personnel and visitors of businesses, government or other organizations or members of private households. Taxi and limousine drivers are employed by taxi and other transportation service companies, or they may be self-employed. Chauffeurs are employed by businesses, government and other organizations, or private individuals or families.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Taxi and limousine drivers and chauffeurs
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Taxi and limousine drivers drive automobiles and limousines to transport passengers.
main_duties: Pick up passengers and drive them to destinations in taxicabs or limousines
Help passengers with luggage and with boarding and exiting vehicles and assist passengers with special needs
Collect flat-rate or taximeter fares
Maintain travel logs and record cash and credit transactions
Maintain contact with taxi dispatch unit
Clean and make minor repairs to vehicle or take vehicle for servicing
May provide pick up and delivery services on request.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
A minimum of one year of safe driving experience is usually required.
A Class G driver's licence is required in Ontario, and a Class 4 driver's licence is required in all other provinces and the territories.
Taxi and limousine drivers require good knowledge of the geographical area to be covered and may have to pass written street/building location and safety examinations.
Taxi drivers usually require a municipal permit.
First aid certification may be required.
additional_information: Mobility from taxi or limousine driver to chauffeur is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Taxi and limousine drivers and chauffeurs
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Chauffeurs drive automobiles and limousines to transport personnel and visitors of businesses, government or other organizations or members of private households.
main_duties: Pick up or meet employer according to request, appointment or schedule
Drive employer to destinations in automobile or limousine
Perform business and personal errands for employer such as delivering and picking up mail, business documents and parcels
Clean and make minor repairs to vehicle or take vehicle for servicing.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
A minimum of one year of safe driving experience is usually required.
A Class G driver's licence is required in Ontario, and a Class 4 driver's licence is required in all other provinces and the territories.
Taxi and limousine drivers require good knowledge of the geographical area to be covered and may have to pass written street/building location and safety examinations.
First aid certification may be required.

units.title: Delivery service drivers and door-to-door distributors
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.minor_group_title: Taxi, personal service and delivery service drivers
units.definition: Delivery service drivers drive automobiles, vans and light trucks to pick up and deliver various products. Door-to-door distributors deliver items to residences and businesses. They are employed by dairies, drug stores, newspaper distributors, take-out food establishments, dry cleaners, mobile caterers and many other establishments, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Delivery service drivers and door-to-door distributors
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Delivery service drivers and door-to-door distributors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Delivery service drivers drive automobiles, vans and light trucks to pick up and deliver various products. Door-to-door distributors deliver items to residences and businesses.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school is usually required.
A driver's licence appropriate to the class of vehicle being driven is required.
One year of safe driving experience is usually required.
On-the-job training may be provided.
Eligibility for bonding and transportation of dangerous goods (TGD) certification may be required for delivery and courier drivers.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions or to non-driving occupations, such as driver trainer or dispatcher is possible with additional training or experience.

core_competencies.statement: to recognize basic cues in clients' body language and tone, adjusting interactions to ensure a comfortable, professional and client-centered delivery experience
to focus on basic scheduling and punctuality, ensuring timely deliveries by organizing routes, prioritizing tasks and minimizing idle time between stops
to perceive their location in relation to the surrounding environment and understand where objects, routes and destinations are relatively
to monitor surroundings effectively, detecting and responding to vehicles, pedestrians and unexpected obstacles on both sides of the vehicle while maintaining focus on the road ahead
to handle moderate stress, staying calm and focused when facing time pressures, traffic challenges, or negative customer interactions
to understand basic route planning, package handling and inventory tracking, ensuring timely deliveries and efficient use of transportation resources

units.title: Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupations
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.minor_group_title: Water and rail transport operators and labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Boat and cable ferry operators and workers in related occupations operate lock gates, bridges and similar equipment along canal systems and operate cable ferries and ferry terminals. This unit group also includes boat operators, and owner-operators, who operate small motorboats or watercraft to transport passengers or freight. They are employed by the federal government, cable ferry companies, ferry terminals, marine companies and canal, port or harbour authorities. Owner-operators of small boats are self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Lock equipment operators operate lock gates, bridges and similar equipment along canal systems.
main_duties: Operate telecommunication equipment or use portable loudspeakers to direct movements of vessels in lock area
Operate controls to open or close lock gates
Operate controls to raise, lower or turn bridges
Observe progress of vessels passing through locks.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: There is little or no mobility among lock equipment operators, cable ferry operators and ferry terminal workers.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Cable ferry operators operate cable ferries.
main_duties: Signal passengers and motor vehicles to embark and disembark
Operate cable ferry across narrow waterway
Perform routine maintenance and repairs to engine, cables and winches.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: There is little or no mobility among lock equipment operators, cable ferry operators and ferry terminal workers.
Mobility between boat operators in this unit group is possible.
Progression from boat operator positions to deck officer positions is possible with additional training, experience and certification.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Ferry terminal workers operate equipment required to perform work at ferry terminals.
main_duties: Operate controls to adjust landing bridge, position or remove gangplank, open or close doors and gates, and secure or remove docking devices
Signal passengers and motor vehicles to embark and disembark
May collect tickets or fares from passengers.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.
additional_information: There is little or no mobility among lock equipment operators, cable ferry operators and ferry terminal workers.
Mobility between boat operators in this unit group is possible.
Progression from boat operator positions to deck officer positions is possible with additional training, experience and certification.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Boat operators, and owner-operators, operate small motorboats or watercraft to transport passengers or freight.
main_duties: Operate motorboats, launches, small ferry boats and other similar vessels to transport passengers or freight
Maintain boats and equipment on board, such as engines, winches, derricks, fire extinguishers and life preservers
May perform other duties, such as checking for oil spills or other pollutants around ports and harbours, patrolling beaches and measuring depth of water.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided.
Master, Master Limited or Small Vessel Operator Proficiency (SVOP) Certificate, issued by Transport Canada, is required for boat operators.
Marine Emergency Duties (MED) training is required for boat operators and crew members.
additional_information: There is little or no mobility among lock equipment operators, cable ferry operators and ferry terminal workers.
Mobility between boat operators in this unit group is possible.
Progression from boat operator positions to deck officer positions is possible with additional training, experience and certification.
workplaces_employers: Marine companies that provide sightseeing tours or water taxi services

units.title: Railway and motor transport labourers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.minor_group_title: Water and rail transport operators and labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Railway and motor transport labourers perform a variety of tasks to assist track maintenance workers and railway yard workers, or motor transport operators. They are employed by railway transport companies and motor transport companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Railway labourers perform a variety of tasks to assist track maintenance workers and railway yard workers.
main_duties: Transport tools and equipment on push or hand car and perform other duties to assist railway track maintenance workers to lay, maintain and repair railway tracks
Cut vegetation growth from railway tracks using scythes and mowers
Check freight cars for physical damage and cleanliness.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
additional_information: With experience, railway labourers may progress to railway track maintenance workers or railway yard workers.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Motor transport labourers perform a variety of tasks to assist motor transport operators.
main_duties: Assist truck and delivery drivers to load and unload vehicles
Perform labouring duties in warehouses.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.

units.title: Public works and maintenance labourers
units.category: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.broad_category_title: Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourers
units.minor_group_title: Water and rail transport operators and labourers and related occupations
units.definition: Public works and maintenance labourers perform a variety of labouring activities to maintain sidewalks, streets, roads and similar areas. They are employed by public works departments in all levels of government or by private contractors under contract to governments.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Transport and heavy equipment operators and servicers (74, 75)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Public works and maintenance labourers perform a variety of labouring activities to maintain sidewalks, streets, roads and similar areas.
employment_requirements: Several weeks of on-the-job training are provided.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions or to public works maintenance equipment operator positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Private contractors under contract to governments
Public works departments in all levels of government

example_titles.job_title_text: line-painting machine operator - highways and roads

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to perform scheduled inspections on machinery, such as excavators or road sweepers, checking for wear and tear, fluid levels and operational performance, document findings and report issues
to operate construction and maintenance equipment (such as backhoes and street sweepers) safely for basic public infrastructure work
to perform sustained manual tasks such as lifting heavy materials, operating infrastructure maintenance machinery and working long hours under varying weather conditions
to shovel cement and other materials into cement mixers, to spread concrete and asphalt on road surfaces using shovels, rakes and hand tampers
to practice teamwork, such as assisting equipment operators to secure attachments to equipment and trucks, clear communication and cooperative problem-solving skills

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Safety and Security
core_competencies.statement: to understand basic safety protocols, hazard identification and emergency response procedures relevant to maintaining public infrastructure and ensuring public safety
